perItem.jp: 読めば読むほど面白い
ここで諦めるわけにはいかない
寿司を食べたことがあります。
子供の頃はよくここで遊んだ。

proposals.title: Make NounPart carry a noun subclass (common/proper/pronoun) instead of a bare string
proposals.rationale: The single most frequent systemic issue: ~10 of 40 issues (r5-a0, r5-a1) are D1/D6 flags that NounPart<N extends string> erases the noun subclass. The library already defines value-identical CommonNoun/ProperNoun/Pronoun aliases, but NounPart only accepts a string, so a pronoun like ここ and formal noun こと are filed as generic nouns and the POS distinction is lost in the type. Fixing the constructor lifts every annotate item containing a pronoun or non-default noun at once.
proposals.change: In /Users/evan/code/typed-japanese/src/phrase-types.d.ts, add a subclass tag to NounPart, e.g. NounPart<N extends string = string, Sub extends "common"|"proper"|"pronoun"|"formal" = "common"> with a nounClass: Sub field (value stays N). Optionally add a dedicated PronounPart alias for ここ/これ/私. Keep value identical so resolution (D5) is unaffected.

proposals.title: Forbid burying demonstrative+particle and わけにはいかない grammar in raw literals
proposals.rationale: r5-c3 (ここで諦めるわけにはいかない) generated ~13 high-severity D2/D3/D6 flags from ONE snippet: ここで collapses ここ+で, and わけにはいかない collapses わけ+に+は+いかない with three particles buried. The pattern (demonstrative + で, formal noun わけ + particles + 行く negative) recurs, so an explicit prompt rule prevents the model from emitting opaque leaves instead of fixing each chapter individually.
proposals.change: In playground/scripts/annotate.ts hard rules, add: demonstrative pronouns (ここ/そこ/あそこ) followed by a particle MUST split into a Pronoun NounPart + ParticlePart (ここで → ここ + で). Formal-noun patterns like わけ/はず/つもり + に/は MUST expose the formal noun and each particle as separate parts; never freeze わけにはいかない as one literal. Extend the existing buried-particle validator to fire on で/に/は after a kana demonstrative.

proposals.title: Never bury a modellable head verb inside a SuffixPart; route ない/ません through the conjugator
proposals.rationale: r5-a2 (行かざるを得ない) produced ~12 high-severity D1/D2/D4/D6 flags because SuffixPart<"行かざる"> hides godan verb 行く (whose 未然形 行か the conjugator produces via Nai), and 得ない is split as VerbPart<得る,"MasuStem"> + SuffixPart<"ない"> instead of ConjugateVerb<得る,"Nai">. Same class as the retired trailing-ます error: a verb the library CAN conjugate must come from VerbPart, with only the genuinely unsupported classical ず/ざる as a residual suffix.
proposals.change: In annotate.ts hard rules, add: a SuffixPart string must NEVER contain kanji belonging to a conjugatable verb stem. Negation ない and polite negation come from ConjugateVerb<V,"Nai">/"Masen", not a trailing literal — use VerbPart<得る,"Nai"> for 得ない. Only the classical ず/ざる (and other unmodeled bound auxiliaries) may be a SuffixPart, attached AFTER a VerbPart<V,"Nai"> that exposes the lexical verb.

proposals.title: Add a classical negative auxiliary (ず / 連体形 ざる) conjugation form
proposals.rationale: Minor: a few r5-a2 D4 notes correctly observe the classical negative ず (連体形 ざる) attaching to 未然形 has no constructor, so ざる legitimately falls back to a SuffixPart. A genuine but narrow gap (古文 negatives); the bigger win is not burying 行く with it (covered above). Worth a small ts-def addition only if classical forms recur.
proposals.change: In /Users/evan/code/typed-japanese/src/verb-types.d.ts, add a classical-negative form (e.g. "ZaruAttributive") mapping the Nai stem + ざる (行か→行かざる), so ざるを得ない can use VerbPart<行く,"ZaruAttributive"> instead of a literal. Lower priority than the constructor and prompt fixes.

proposals.title: Use ParticlePart<"ほど"> for the 〜ば〜ほど construction in r5-c0
proposals.rationale: r5-c0 (読めば読むほど面白い): low-severity D3/D6 — ほど is a 副助詞 already in the Particle union and ParticlePart but is left as a raw literal. One-off authoring fix; low impact but trivial and honest.
proposals.change: In the chapter snippet for 読めば読むほど面白い, replace the raw ほど literal with ParticlePart<"ほど">. The bare ば after the Hypothetical stem may stay.

issues.note: ほど is a 副助詞 and the library defines Particle 'ほど' / ParticlePart; it is left as a raw literal in the template instead of a typed particle leaf.
issues.jp: 読めば読むほど面白い

issues.note: The 〜ば〜ほど construction's particles (ば/れば and ほど) are modellable via Particle/ConditionalParticle but expressed as raw string literals, slightly less idiomatic than a teacher's segmentation. The bare ば appended after the Hypothetical stem is defensible, but ほど has a dedicated type.
issues.jp: 読めば読むほど面白い

issues.note: Particle で in ここで is buried in a literal; should be ここ (Pronoun/demonstrative) + で particle as separate units.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: Particles に and は inside わけにはいかない are buried; library has particle constructors to expose them.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: ここで and わけにはいかない collapse multiple morphemes into single opaque literals, hiding internal structure.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: いかない is the negative form of 行く and could be expressed via the conjugator rather than a raw literal.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: ここ (demonstrative pronoun) and いかない (verb) receive no constructor/POS; only 諦める is modeled.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: Particles で, に, は are buried in the literals ここで and わけにはいかない instead of being visible particle units.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: わけにはいかない collapses わけ(noun)+に+は+いかない(verb) into one opaque leaf; ここで collapses ここ+で.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: いかない is the negative (未然形+ない) of Godan verb 行く and could be expressed via the conjugator (ConjugateVerb<行く,"Nai">) rather than frozen in a literal.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: Only the single verb 諦める is modeled; the rest of the sentence including a conjugated verb and three particles is left as raw text, not matching idiomatic analysis.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: ここ (demonstrative pronoun) gets no Pronoun constructor; left inside a literal.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: Particles で (in ここで) and に/は (in わけにはいかない) are buried in raw literals; particle integrity broken. ここで at minimum should expose で.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: 'ここで' collapses ここ + で and 'わけにはいかない' collapses わけ + に + は + いかない into single leaves; only 諦める is decomposed.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: ここ (pronoun/demonstrative) and the formal noun わけ are not placed in any noun constructor; they are hidden in literals.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: いかない is the Negative (ナイ) form of 行く and is modellable via ConjugateVerb, but it is frozen as a literal; defensible only if treating わけにはいかない as a fixed idiom.
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: わけにはいかない is reasonably kept whole as a fixed pattern, but ここで is plain grammar and should be decomposed (ここ + で).
issues.jp: ここで諦めるわけにはいかない

issues.note: 寿司 and こと are 普通名詞 but use bare NounPart<"...">, hiding the noun subclass. Library has CommonNoun, but NounPart only accepts a string, so the common-noun label is lost. こと here is also a 形式名詞 (formal noun); CommonNoun is the closest available wrapper.
issues.jp: 寿司を食べたことがあります。

issues.note: こと is a 形式名詞 (formal/dependent noun) and 寿司 a 普通名詞; NounPart cannot distinguish common vs formal-noun subclass, so the analysis is correct but the distinction is not surfaced in the type.
issues.jp: 寿司を食べたことがあります。

issues.note: 寿司 and こと go through POS-agnostic NounPart, which cannot record the common-noun (普通名詞) / 形式名詞 subclass; a subclass-tagged noun constructor would preserve information NounPart erases.
issues.jp: 寿司を食べたことがあります。

issues.note: ここ is a 指示代名詞 (demonstrative pronoun) but is wrapped in NounPart, which is POS-agnostic and exposes no Pronoun subclass; the library lacks a PronounPart, so the pronoun POS is lost.
issues.jp: 子供の頃はよくここで遊んだ。

issues.note: 子供 and 頃 are 普通名詞 but NounPart cannot record the common-noun subclass (no CommonNoun distinction), so the noun subclass is not preserved even though the choice is defensible.
issues.jp: 子供の頃はよくここで遊んだ。

issues.note: Treating ここ as a generic noun rather than a demonstrative pronoun is mildly non-idiomatic; a teacher would label it 代名詞.
issues.jp: 子供の頃はよくここで遊んだ。

issues.note: ここ is a 代名詞 (demonstrative pronoun) but is filed under NounPart, which is POS-agnostic and exposes no pronoun subclass; the library has a Pronoun<> alias but no dedicated PronounPart phrase constructor, so the pronoun distinction is lost.
issues.jp: 子供の頃はよくここで遊んだ。

issues.note: 子供 and 頃 are 普通名詞 but NounPart does not record the common-noun subclass; no CommonNounPart exists to preserve it.
issues.jp: 子供の頃はよくここで遊んだ。

issues.note: ここ is a demonstrative pronoun (代名詞) but is wrapped in NounPart, which is POS-agnostic and offers no PronounPart constructor, so the pronoun subclass is silently lost. 子供/頃 as common nouns suffer the same agnostic-noun limitation but are at least the right broad category.
issues.jp: 子供の頃はよくここで遊んだ。

issues.note: SuffixPart<"行かざる"> buries the content verb 行く (godan, 未然形 行か) plus the classical negative auxiliary ず in 連体形 ざる. 行く is fully modellable as a GodanVerb (its Nai/未然形 stem is か), so the verb should not be hidden in a suffix string. Only the classical ず→ざる auxiliary is genuinely unsupported.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: 行かざる is a single leaf collapsing a verb stem and an auxiliary; no internal decomposition into 行か + ざる.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: The classical negative ず (連体形 ざる) attaching to a 未然形 has no constructor in the library, forcing the literal. The polite/Nai paradigm does not cover 古文 negatives.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: 得 is produced via VerbPart<得る,"MasuStem"> but the negative ない attaches to the 未然形. For ichidan both MasuStem and Nai resolve to bare 得, so surface is fine, but the form label misrepresents the morphology; ConjugateVerb<得る,"Nai"> would be the honest choice (also yields 得).
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: 得ない is split as VerbPart(MasuStem) + SuffixPart<"ない">; since the library's ichidan Nai form only yields the bare stem 得 (not 得ない), the trailing ない literal is unavoidable, but the analysis would read more idiomatically with the Nai form labeled rather than MasuStem.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: 行く is a standard godan verb the library models; freezing 行か into SuffixPart<"行かざる"> hides the verb POS. The verb should be a VerbPart (行く mizenkei) with only the classical ざる auxiliary as a suffix.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: 行か(未然形)+ざる are merged into one leaf. The modern paradigm lacks a 未然形/ざる auxiliary form, so the morpheme split cannot be fully expressed — but 行く itself should still surface as a verb rather than the whole span being one suffix.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: 得ない = ConjugateVerb<得る,"Nai">, directly supported. It is instead MasuStem 得 + raw SuffixPart<"ない">; should be VerbPart<得る,"Nai"> so the negative inflection comes from the conjugator.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: Segmentation misrepresents the ざるを得ない idiom: head verb hidden in a suffix and negation as a detached literal. Even keeping the fixed expression whole would be more honest than this split.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: 行く is buried inside SuffixPart<"行かざる">. It should be VerbPart<行く,"Nai"> (resolves to 行か) plus a separate SuffixPart<"ざる"> for the classical negative auxiliary ず, which the verb conjugator does support for the 行か part.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: 行かざる must decompose into 行か (verb) + ざる (auxiliary). Collapsing them hides the verb and the auxiliary morpheme.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: The 〜ざるを得ない pattern's first verb should be exposed as the actual lexical verb (行く); freezing it as a suffix string is actively misleading analysis.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: 得 before ない is the negative base (未然形); the snippet labels it "MasuStem" (連用形). For ichidan both surfaces are 得, so it type-checks, but "Nai" is the correct form name.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。

issues.note: The library has no dedicated classical-negative (ず/ざる) auxiliary form, so ざる legitimately needs a SuffixPart; that part of the analysis is honest. The error is burying 行く along with it.
issues.jp: 行かざるを得ない。
